setup.context: Building a contacts app that stores names and phone numbers

root.narration: You are building a contacts app! You need to store people's names AND their phone numbers, so when someone searches for 'Mom' the app shows her phone number. A regular list just stores items in numbered spots. You need something that connects a NAME to a PHONE NUMBER. What data structure should you use?

choices.text: A dictionary! It stores pairs: each name (the key) is connected to a phone number (the value). Look up 'Mom' and get her number instantly.
Two separate lists: one for names and one for phone numbers. Hope they stay lined up.
One big list with names and numbers all mixed together.

outcome.headline: Dictionary! Keys and Values Paired Together!
outcome.explanation: A DICTIONARY (also called a map or hash map) stores KEY-VALUE pairs! The key is what you look up ('Mom') and the value is what you get back ('555-1234'). It works just like a real dictionary: you look up a WORD (key) and find its DEFINITION (value). This is perfect for contacts because you always search by name to find a number. Instant lookup!
outcome.whatToRemember: A dictionary stores key-value pairs. Use it when you need to look something up by name and get associated information back.

keyTerms.definition: A data structure that stores pairs of keys and values. Look up the key to get the value, like looking up a word to find its definition.
Two connected pieces of information: the key is what you search for, the value is what you find.

outcome.headline: Fragile! What If They Get Out of Sync?
outcome.explanation: Two lists could work IF 'Mom' is in spot 3 of the names list AND her number is in spot 3 of the numbers list. But what if you delete a name and forget to delete the matching number? Everything shifts and now names point to wrong numbers! A dictionary keeps each name permanently glued to its number -- they can never get separated.
outcome.whatToRemember: Keeping related data in separate lists is risky -- they can get out of sync. Dictionaries keep paired data together safely.

outcome.headline: Which Number Belongs to Which Person?
outcome.explanation: If names and numbers are all mixed in one list like ['Mom', '555-1234', 'Dad', '555-5678'], how does the computer know which number belongs to which person? It cannot tell! Is '555-1234' a name or a number? Choosing the right data structure means picking the container that keeps your data organized in a way that makes sense for how you will use it.
outcome.whatToRemember: Choose data structures that match how you will USE the data. Contacts need name-to-number lookup, so use a dictionary!
